notes: Airfare maps directly to Travel.
The flight fare already matched the stamped total.
OCR was clean on the first pass.
Pens, markers, and paper were the dominant line items.
The fallback extraction pass resolved the missing critical fields.
A targeted retry recovered the store name and the total from the footer.
Focused retry hint: vendor, total.
The agent needed a second extraction attempt because the invoice image is intentionally noisy.
The document quality is poor, so the result should stay flagged for manual spot checking.
Hotel charges map directly to Travel.
The folio total was already normalized.
The hotel folio is legible, but it does not show an invoice number.
Invoice number is absent in the source image, so the report leaves it blank.
The invoice is a clear software subscription purchase.
Line items were normalized to the stamped invoice total.
The multi-line subscription bundle is still easy to total in mock mode.
The invoice is clearly for package shipment charges.
Normalization preferred the stamped total over the line-item subtotal because the surcharge was corrected by hand.
The subtotal and the stamped total conflict in the source image.
Totals conflict in the source document, so normalization had to choose the stamped amount.
The stamped amount was treated as the source of truth when totals conflicted.
Meals & Entertainment is the most plausible category, but the business purpose is ambiguous.
The line items were already summarized into a single total.
The document looks like a restaurant invoice for a team dinner.
Conservative mode moved this invoice to Other because the categorization confidence stayed below 0.75.
The invoice is ambiguous enough that a conservative reviewer may want to re-check the category.
The invoice does not say whether the meal was customer-facing or internal.
